info.title: Zendesk Support API
info.description:
The Zendesk Support API (also known as the Ticketing API) provides RESTful access to Zendesk Support resources such as tickets, users, organizations, groups, and more. It enables developers to build custom integrations, automate workflows, and manage customer support operations programmatically.

Capabilities include:
- Ticket management - Create, update, delete, and search tickets
- User management - Manage end users, agents, and administrators
- Organization management - Segment customers into organizations
- Group management - Organize agents into groups for ticket routing
- Ticket fields and forms - Customize ticket data collection
- Views - Create and manage filtered ticket lists
- Macros - Define reusable sets of ticket actions
- Triggers and automations - Automate ticket workflows
- SLA policies - Define service level agreements
- Satisfaction ratings - Track customer satisfaction
- Tags - Categorize and organize resources
- Search - Unified search across tickets, users, and organizations
- Attachments - Upload and manage file attachments
- Brands - Manage multiple brands
- Job statuses - Track bulk and background operations

get.description: Returns a list of tickets. Tickets are ordered chronologically by created date, from oldest to newest. The first ticket listed may not be the oldest ticket in your account due to ticket archiving.
Returns the ticket with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the ticket with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the ticket with the specified ID.

get.description: Accepts a comma-separated list of ticket IDs to return.

post.description: Accepts an array of up to 100 ticket objects. Returns a job status to track the progress.

put.description: Accepts an array of up to 100 ticket objects. Returns a job status.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of ticket IDs to update (for bulk updates with the same values).

delete.description: Accepts a comma-separated list of up to 100 ticket IDs to delete.

get.description: Returns an approximate count of tickets in the account. Refreshed every 100 seconds.
Returns the comments added to a ticket.

parameters.name: include_inline_images
parameters.description: Include inline images in the response.

put.description: Changes a comment from public to private.

get.description: Returns a list of all ticket fields in your account, including both system and custom fields.
Returns the ticket field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket field.

put.description: Updates the ticket field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket field.

delete.description: Deletes the ticket field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket field.

get.description: Returns a list of all ticket forms for your account.
Returns the ticket form with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_form_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket form.

put.description: Updates the ticket form with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_form_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket form.

delete.description: Deletes the ticket form with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ticket_form_id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket form.

get.description: Returns the metrics for a specific ticket.
Returns ticket metrics for the most recent 100 tickets.
Returns a list of all users in your account. Agents and admins can access this endpoint.

parameters.name: permission_set
parameters.description: Filter by permission set for custom agent roles.

get.description: Returns the user with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the user with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the user with the specified ID (soft delete). The user is marked as deleted and can no longer sign in.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user.
Accepts a comma-separated list of user IDs or external IDs.

post.description: Accepts an array of up to 100 user objects.
Creates or updates a user. If a user with the specified email or external ID already exists, the user is updated.

get.description: Returns an approximate count of users in the account.
Returns tickets requested by the specified user.
Returns tickets assigned to the specified user.
Returns a list of all custom user fields.
Returns the user field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: user_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the user field.

put.description: Updates the user field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: user_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the user field.

delete.description: Deletes the user field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: user_field_id
parameters.description: The ID of the user field.

get.description: Returns a list of identities for the specified user.

post.description: Creates a new identity for the specified user.

get.description: Returns the identity with the specified ID for the specified user.

delete.description: Deletes the identity with the specified ID.

put.description: Sets the specified identity as the primary identity for the user.

get.description: Returns a list of organizations.
Returns the organization with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the organization with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns an approximate count of organizations.
Returns matching organizations for autocomplete dropdowns.
Accepts a comma-separated list of organization IDs or external IDs.
Returns a list of all custom organization fields.
Returns a list of all organization memberships.
Returns the organization membership with the specified ID.

parameters.name: membership_id
parameters.description: The ID of the organization membership.

delete.description: Deletes the organization membership with the specified ID.

parameters.name: membership_id
parameters.description: The ID of the organization membership.

get.description: Returns a list of groups.
Returns the group with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the group with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the group with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns an approximate count of groups.
Returns a list of group memberships.
Returns the group membership with the specified ID.

parameters.name: membership_id
parameters.description: The ID of the group membership.

delete.description: Deletes the group membership with the specified ID.

parameters.name: membership_id
parameters.description: The ID of the group membership.

get.description: Returns search results across tickets, users, and organizations. Results are limited to 1,000 items and sorted by relevance by default.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query. See the Zendesk search reference for query syntax.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort by a specific attribute (e.g., created_at, updated_at, priority, status, ticket_type).

get.description: Returns the count of results matching the search query without returning actual results.
Lists shared and personal views available to the current user.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort by attribute (e.g., alphabetical, created_at, updated_at).

get.description: Returns the view with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the view with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the view with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns a list of active shared views.
Returns tickets matching the view's conditions.
Returns the ticket count for the specified view.
Returns a list of macros accessible to the current user.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Include related records (e.g., usage_7d, usage_24h, usage_30d).

get.description: Returns the macro with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the macro with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the macro with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns the changes a macro would make to a ticket without actually applying them.
Returns a list of all triggers for the current account.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort by attribute (e.g., alphabetical, created_at, updated_at, position).

get.description: Returns the trigger with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the trigger with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the trigger with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns a list of all automations for the current account.
Returns the automation with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the automation with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the automation with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns a list of all SLA policies.
Returns the SLA policy with the specified ID.

parameters.name: sla_policy_id
parameters.description: The ID of the SLA policy.

put.description: Updates the SLA policy with the specified ID.

parameters.name: sla_policy_id
parameters.description: The ID of the SLA policy.

delete.description: Deletes the SLA policy with the specified ID.

parameters.name: sla_policy_id
parameters.description: The ID of the SLA policy.

get.description: Returns a list of all satisfaction ratings.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Return ratings created after this time (Unix epoch time).

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Return ratings created before this time (Unix epoch time).

get.description: Returns the satisfaction rating with the specified ID.

parameters.name: satisfaction_rating_id
parameters.description: The ID of the satisfaction rating.

post.description: Creates a satisfaction rating for a solved ticket.

get.description: Returns the tags on a ticket.

put.description: Replaces all tags on a ticket with the specified tags.

post.description: Adds tags to a ticket without removing existing tags.

delete.description: Removes specified tags from a ticket.

get.description: Returns a list of all tags used in the account.

post.description: Uploads a file to be attached to a ticket comment. The response includes a token that can be used to attach the upload to a ticket.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The name of the file being uploaded.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: An existing upload token to add files to a previous upload.

delete.description: Deletes a previously uploaded file that has not yet been attached to a comment.

get.description: Returns the attachment with the specified ID.

delete.description: Redacts the attachment with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns a list of all brands for the account.
Returns the brand with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the brand with the specified ID.

delete.description: Deletes the brand with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns a list of tickets that have been suspended.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort by attribute (e.g., author_email, cause, created_at, subject).

get.description: Returns the suspended ticket with the specified ID.

parameters.name: suspended_ticket_id
parameters.description: The ID of the suspended ticket.

delete.description: Deletes the suspended ticket with the specified ID.

parameters.name: suspended_ticket_id
parameters.description: The ID of the suspended ticket.

put.description: Recovers a suspended ticket and creates a new ticket from it.

parameters.name: suspended_ticket_id
parameters.description: The ID of the suspended ticket.

get.description: Returns the status of a background job.

parameters.name: job_status_id
parameters.description: The ID of the job status.

get.description: Returns a list of job statuses.
Accepts a comma-separated list of job status IDs.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of job status IDs.

get.description: Returns requests for the currently authenticated end user. Agents can also use this endpoint.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status (open, solved, ccd, etc.).

post.description: Creates a request on behalf of the end user.

get.description: Returns the request with the specified ID.

put.description: Updates the request with the specified ID.

get.description: Returns comments on the specified request.
Returns a list of all custom ticket statuses.

parameters.name: status_category
parameters.description: Filter by status category (new, open, pending, hold, solved).

get.description: Returns the custom status with the specified ID.

parameters.name: custom_status_id
parameters.description: The ID of the custom status.

put.description: Updates the custom status with the specified ID.

parameters.name: custom_status_id
parameters.description: The ID of the custom status.

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication. Zendesk supports Authorization Code, Implicit, and Resource Owner Password Credentials flows. OAuth tokens are more secure than API tokens because they use scopes to limit access and can be easily revoked.

basicAuth.description: Basic authentication using email/password. Note: Password-based basic authentication is being deprecated by Zendesk. Use API token or OAuth instead.

apiTokenAuth.description: API token authentication using basic auth with the format {email_address}/token:{api_token}. API tokens are auto-generated from the Zendesk Admin Center. You can have up to 256 active tokens per account. Encode the credentials as base64 and use the Authorization: Basic header.

PerPageParam.name: per_page
PerPageParam.description: The number of results per page (max 100).

SortByParam.name: sort_by
SortByParam.description: Sort by attribute (e.g., created_at, updated_at, priority, status).

Unauthorized.description: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found.

UnprocessableEntity.description: The request was well-formed but contained semantic errors.

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit exceeded. The response includes a Retry-After header indicating when to retry.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying.

next_page.description: URL to the next page of results.

previous_page.description: URL to the previous page of results.

Ticket.description: A ticket represents a customer support interaction.

external_id.description: External ID for linking to an external system.

raw_subject.description: The raw subject, without any ticket-related prefixes.

description.description: The first comment on the ticket (read-only).

custom_status_id.description: The custom status ID of the ticket.

recipient.description: The original recipient email address of the ticket.

requester_id.description: The ID of the user who requested the ticket.

submitter_id.description: The ID of the user who submitted the ticket.

assignee_id.description: The ID of the agent assigned to the ticket.

organization_id.description: The ID of the organization of the requester.

group_id.description: The ID of the group assigned to the ticket.

collaborator_ids.description: IDs of users CC'd on the ticket.

follower_ids.description: IDs of agents following the ticket.

email_cc_ids.description: IDs of agents or end users CC'd on the ticket.

forum_topic_id.description: The topic in the Zendesk Web portal this ticket originated from.

problem_id.description: For incidents, the ID of the problem the incident is linked to.

has_incidents.description: Whether the ticket has been marked as a problem with linked incidents.

is_public.description: Whether the ticket is publicly visible.

due_at.description: If the ticket is of type 'task', the due date.

tags.description: The tags applied to the ticket.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values on the ticket.

satisfaction_rating.description: The satisfaction rating of the ticket.

ticket_form_id.description: Enterprise only. The ID of the ticket form.

brand_id.description: Enterprise only. The ID of the brand.

updated_at.description: When the ticket was last updated.

TicketCreateInput.description: Input object for creating a ticket.

public.description: Whether the comment is public (true) or internal (false).

author_id.description: The ID of the comment author.

CommentInput.description: Input object for creating a comment on a ticket.

Via.description: How the ticket or event was created.

channel.description: The channel (e.g., web, email, api, chat, phone, twitter, facebook).

id.description: The ID of the custom field.

value.description: The value of the custom field.

type.description: The type of the ticket field.

TicketForm.description: A ticket form that defines which fields are shown.

User.description: A user in Zendesk (end user, agent, or admin).

UserInput.description: Input object for creating a user.

CustomField.description: A custom field definition (for users or organizations).

UserIdentity.description: A user identity (email, phone, twitter, etc.).

Organization.description: An organization that groups end users.

OrganizationMembership.description: A membership associating a user with an organization.

GroupMembership.description: A membership associating an agent with a group.

View.description: A view defining filtered ticket lists.

Macro.description: A macro for applying predefined ticket actions.

Trigger.description: A trigger that automatically executes actions on ticket events.

Automation.description: An automation that runs on a time-based schedule.

token.description: Token to use when attaching to a ticket comment.

SuspendedTicket.description: A ticket that has been flagged and suspended.

Request.description: A request from the end-user perspective.
